facebook.content_angle: Turns in the pool — the race-within-the-race that most club swimmers treat as a rest break instead of a weapon
facebook.post_text:
Your turns are costing you more than you think. 🔄

Most swimmers treat the wall like a checkpoint. Elite swimmers treat it like a slingshot. If you're barely using your push-off, you're handing back metres you worked hard to earn.

👉 A weak turn can cost you 0.3–0.8 seconds per wall — across 8 turns in a 200m race, that's a different heat entirely
👉 The fix starts before you touch the wall — your approach stroke, your tuck speed, and your foot placement all determine how much power you generate off the push
👉 Drill: Swim into the wall at race pace, push off in streamline, hold it until you reach 5m — if you surface before the flags, your push-off needs work

The wall isn't a break. It's where races are won. 🏆

✔️ Time your turns during training — not just your lap times
✔️ Practise explosive tuck rotations on every rep, not just time trials
✔️ Treat every single wall in every session like race day

How many walls do you swim in your main set each session?
facebook.engagement_bait: Drop your main set distance below — let's see how many walls you're leaving on the table.
facebook.call_to_action: How many walls do you swim in your main set each session?
